title: Supply Chain, Browser Binaries, Packages, Containers, and Rollout · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers supply chain, browser binaries, packages, containers, and rollout into a changed source-bound least-authority crawler release. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of source-bound least-authority crawler release with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership.
Bind Playwright package, browser revision, download source, digest, platform libraries, sandbox policy, and patch process.
Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Dependency Provenance
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1683 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove dependency and browser provenance, package contents, least-authority containers, and immutable rollout; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
